page: Amazon Web Services m8i.96xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 384 dedicated vCPUs derived from 192 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The server does not include local storage or GPUs, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in multi-core operations, database tasks, and memory bandwidth. The multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and PassMark CPU score rank in the top 10%, aided by advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 30541.88 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 1145.17 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier throughput. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds represent a relative tradeoff, with single-core stress-ng and small-model text generation scoring in the average and lower tiers respectively.

Given its high resource density and lack of bundled local storage, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale, memory-intensive compute workloads that rely on network-attached storage. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, high-throughput database operations, static web serving, and prompt processing phases of language models.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.96xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 384 dedicated vCPUs from 192 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, delivering a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides 100 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database throughput, supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Conversely, single-core performance and text generation speeds represent relative performance tradeoffs. This server is highly suited for enterprise database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, high-volume static web serving, and large-scale general-purpose compute workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.96xlarge is a general-purpose server with 384 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.96xlarge features 384 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps bandwidth for high-throughput database and multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose server featuring 384 vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 384 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Supports advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Best suited for enterprise databases, software compilation, and multi-threaded applications
